We, as long-term freelancing riders of Deliveroo, are sorry to inform the public about the fiasco of the delivery company’s newly introduced policy change surrounding our incomes, already to cause a sensation in social media and networking, including our customers. Of which, the company has failed its promise to deduct the delivery fee of short-distancing orders on the one end, and to increase that of long-distancing ones on the other, and which, as 1,400 freelancing contractors amongst us reflect, have not secured, if not boosted, our total incomes as planned.
 
            The delivery fees, no matter with any distance, have plummeted to a new low, and which, as many reckon, seems to be the company’s scheme to wax our revenues, in spite of what it has claimed in the first place. But above all, some of us have no orders at all for a whole day and, with the company’s recent abjuration of “guaranteed minimum wage”, we have no income at all, despite the company has required us to stand outside restaurants in the sun for a better chance at receiving orders. In the past, the company did terminate the contracts of few freelancers who did not stay close enough to restaurants.
 
            In light of this, many of us across  races and cultures unanimously feel the need to start this non-cooperation movement, not to allow the company to look askance at its problematic operation system. We are hereby making the following demands:
 
1)    All order fees should go back to the normal price level, wherein they should be at least HK$35 for walkers and cyclists, and HK$55 for motorbike riders.
 
2)    “A guaranteed minimum wage” should be restored.
 
3)    A requirement of 80% acceptance rate should be reconsidered.
 
 
Starting from the evening of May 24th, we will take actions involving delaying order deliveries and skives, and which, as we promise, will keep in line with food safety as possible as we can. We are sorry to bring inconveniences to customers, as we estimate there will be a longer waiting time for each order than it should be, whereas we encourage them not to place any orders until the company takes a responsible action to improve working conditions. We will not rule out possibilities to escalate our action to another level, if the company continues to be irresponsive as it is now.
 
Last but not least, we hope to serve our customers again in a near future, whereas everything will be restored to the normal, if not a better, state.
承諾唔加料，唔偷食，唔掟嘢
但係直接冇人送貨

We, as long-term freelancing riders of Deliveroo, are sorry to inform the public about the fiasco of the delivery company’s newly introduced policy change surrounding our incomes, already to cause a sensation in social media and networking, including our customers. Of which, the company has failed its promise to deduct the delivery fee of short-distancing orders on the one end, and to increase that of long-distancing ones on the other, and which, as 1,400 freelancing contractors amongst us reflect, have not secured, if not boosted, our total incomes as planned.
 
            The delivery fees, no matter with any distance, have plummeted to a new low, and which, as many reckon, seems to be the company’s scheme to wax our revenues, in spite of what it has claimed in the first place. But above all, some of us have no orders at all for a whole day and, with the company’s recent abjuration of “guaranteed minimum wage”, we have no income at all, despite the company has required us to stand outside restaurants in the sun for a better chance at receiving orders. In the past, the company did terminate the contracts of few freelancers who did not stay close enough to restaurants.
 
            In light of this, many of us across  races and cultures unanimously feel the need to start this non-cooperation movement, not to allow the company to look askance at its problematic operation system. We are hereby making the following demands:
 
1)    All order fees should go back to the normal price level, wherein they should be at least HK$35 for walkers and cyclists, and HK$55 for motorbike riders.
 
2)    “A guaranteed minimum wage” should be restored.
 
3)    A requirement of 80% acceptance rate should be reconsidered.
 
 
Starting from the evening of May 24th, we will take actions involving delaying order deliveries and skives, and which, as we promise, will keep in line with food safety as possible as we can. We are sorry to bring inconveniences to customers, as we estimate there will be a longer waiting time for each order than it should be, whereas we encourage them not to place any orders until the company takes a responsible action to improve working conditions. We will not rule out possibilities to escalate our action to another level, if the company continues to be irresponsive as it is now.
 
Last but not least, we hope to serve our customers again in a near future, whereas everything will be restored to the normal, if not a better, state.
承諾唔加料，唔偷食，唔掟嘢
但係直接冇人送貨

咁先係正路
個客無得罪你加料偷食唔係玩鳩公司係玩鳩個客

replies.msg: 理性過熊貓好多, 希望大家爭取到自己想要既野
